headline: Sabah Native Court Backlog Hits 12,000 Pending Cases
context: Sabah Native Court system reported backlog of 12,000 cases with average resolution times exceeding 7 years for land disputes.

cards.big: 12,000 cases backlog in Sabah Native Court with 7-year average wait
cards.sub: Only 58 native court judges serve the entire state indigenous justice system

cards.big: State allocated only RM8 million for Native Court operations in 2026
cards.sub: Each judge handles an average of 207 active cases simultaneously

cards.big: 3,400 backlogged cases involve land disputes worth over RM100,000 each
cards.sub: Claimants often die before cases resolved passing disputes to next generation

cards.big: Federal Courts Act does not recognize native court decisions for enforcement
cards.sub: Successful claimants must re-litigate in civil courts adding 3-5 more years

cards.big: Court backlogs test whether indigenous justice systems receive equal institutional support
12,000 pending native court cases mean justice delayed is customary rights systematically denied.
